reviews.review_description: Happy to have found this game for my META QUEST 3s... sorry i was sriting ATARI QUEST 3s because i was teletransported in time to 1978 by Pixel Ripped.. Youcna play monitor inisde monitor and interact with environment it is really COOL! Thank you!!!
Absolutely loved this game !  I wish I was a game developer lol .
Every few minutes it finds another delightfull way to tickle that nostalgia bone

It's not a bad game , it's just I believe that Atari fans will get the most of it. I am too young to have experienced Atari so to me the 1995 version of the game spoke to me much more. Otherwise we have decent graphic and the feeling of playing a retro game inside an old school environment is still present.

What a fantastic game! To me, this is what VR is best at. Transporting us to worlds we can't visit any other way. I've wanted to live in a video game since I was a kid, and this is exactly what I thought it would be like. Add in a great original story and the innovative gameplay we're used to from Pixel Ripped and you easily have the best game in the series. The ending even made me a little emotional. I can't wait to see what's next from Ana and the team! 
We've seen Dot in 8 bit and 16 bit, maybe... Pixel Ripped 64??
I can’t refund it now after like 1 hour and such a waste for $25 like what is this 🫩✌️

I've just started the game and I am already stuck on the centipede portion. I have been trying for 3 hours now but I cannot get past it, and I physically never will be able to. Thus the rest of the game is unplayable and useless. For the life of me I do not understand why these game developers do not include the EXTREMELY BASIC function of skipping any part of the game you do not like or are unable to complete, since you're just blocked there forever and have shelled 30$ for a game you cannot play anymore.

This game is so good. I know the franchise didn't start here, but this was the first one I played. I really liked the way it works, being in the real world playing a video game (inside the game) and also being inside the game itself haha ​​Awesome. The story is also good.

What a great experience it is playing this! Very well done and really clever elements of gameplay 👍 as someone who was born in 1970 it's great to see that era brought to life! I recommend this wholeheartedly 👍

reviews.review_title: Great mix of nostalgia and modern tech
reviews.review_description: I had a lot of fun with game. The mix of 3D worlds and 2D platforming was great and there are lots of charming ideas. On to the other Pixel Ripped games :).

I just love the experience especially since I’m from the era of ATARI, I actually love the throwback design of the game and it’s really fun to play. I recommend it to children of all ages.
 Thanks to the developers for sharing this experience.

Great job!  This is such a creative game with so many cool parts. It’s been a while since I played 1995 but I think I like this one even better. The main issue I had was when I was playing from inside the games. If I would move at all in real life the screen would go black. Really inconvenient during combat. One time the game froze during a boss fight when I was making progress so that sucked but not a big issue.  Oh!  Got SO excited when Wolf called!  That was awesome. The only call I enjoyed :D
Unique and fun game - 1995 equally great too. Up there with the best for proper multi-faceted VR experience

To put it bluntly, it's a celebration of 50 year old games, designed for people who have never touched a game in their life.

The difficulty is so lax that combat might as well have been excluded entirely, and yet the constant stream of instant death traps & unskipable, Neverending cutscenes is enough to put off those younger gamers who light difficulty is designed for.

The game is also unclear about saving, and closing at the wrong time can see you having to complete 10-15+ minutes of content to catch up... with that content likely being more cutscene.

There are some good ideas here, but nothing is capitalized on. We're this a studios first attempt, it might be worth more than 2 stars, but being the third game in the franchise, it's honestly sad.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Each Pixel Ripped is different in its own way, and sometimes these differences respond better with one person but not so much so with another. It is such a shame to hear that the ideas presented in 78 did not resonate with you as much as with other players who loved the game 😕

reviews.review_description:
If you liked the other two, have no fear, you will enjoy this one. It's called 1978, but it doesn't stay in that era. It is the best of the three, the most playable without friction.

GOTO LINK FOR TWENETY-FIVE OFf

Daft.bio.link

ALSO please thumbs up if you found this helpfull

reviews.review_title: I didn't like PR 1995, but I LOVE this
reviews.review_description:
OK, I know PR 1995 is really beloved, but I was not a fan.  I assumed there would be more actual VR FPS type gameplay in it.  As you probably already know, it was mostly platforming 2D gameplay.  Yeah, it gave me a sense of nostalgia and all, but it was basically just playing 2D games in VR.  
The funny thing is that most of the negative reviews I have read about this game was that there is "too much" VR FPS type gameplay.  That is actually why I LOVE this game.  I totally want that VR experience, and it was a really nice balance with the 2D Atari 2600 style gameplay that was mixed in.  
If you want the same PR 1995 experience, then you may be disappointed.   Like I said, you will get a lot of VR action mixed in with the early 80's era 2D gameplay.  It is also fun to be in the office environment, which I am VERY familiar with as someone who works in I.T. and in Silicon Valley, the very same area our protagonists office is located.  So yeah, I have a pretty strong connection to this game.
I hope this helps anyone who may be on the fence about this one.
I Leik it. W

I adore Pixel Ripped 1995. It's my favorite VR game — crafted with passion, cozy, nostalgic, and captivating. I've played it multiple times on Hardcore mode. However, Pixel Ripped 1978 is an entirely different game. Instead of fitting references to old games, you'll encounter a dreary Minecraft, repulsive characters, and the same silly pixel platformer. At some point, I just couldn't bear it and abandoned playing since it's so dull and unappealing.

Enjoyed the other two games. My least favourite part of those was the 3d pixel world adventure type sections. They were boring. However, they seem to have made that the whole game this time. They've added combat but it's like a poor version of Wolfenstein 3d for VR. Definitely not worth the mild motion sickness.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! 
Each Pixel Ripped is different in its own way, and sometimes these differences respond better with one person but not so much so with another, and that's ok in the end. It's a shame 78 didn't vibe with you 😕

reviews.review_description:
Gosh, where do I start. I was really confused in starting this game. Like other people, I loved seeing pixel games and such from my younger years. I really enjoy the desk where your constantly interrupted because of the employees but having the same interruption over an over becomes really annoying. It was funny at first but not the 20th time. I get the idea of going into the game an then out. I don't quite get the time travel. It's pretty irritating when your "playing" the video game from the desk in your office and can't get your bearings when trying to find the golden cartridges. You travel back an forth, up and down and don't really know where your suppose to go. I only enjoyed when you were "in" the game. I feel like I've been playing for days an days but only have 10 of the 40 gold cartridges. Now I'm not excited to keep looking. As I do understand the story, it just jumps around too much. I hope there is something new while looking for the next 10 gold cartridges since this is becoming a bit monotonous. 
   As far as graphics, bad guy, monsters, it's really cool. A bit tired of "reading" characters talking to me but I understand. 
   Hopefully after I'm done with this game (maybe), I will have changed my opinions.  We'll see.....
  I gave 5 stars because I just love that I'm able to play a game in VR and know it takes a great deal to make a game like this. If you were my age, you would understand how it was back then an how lucky you are to have such a "console" to play games on. I started on an Intellivision. Google it..lol

I'm new to the Pixel Ripped series, I grew up playing Atari and still do. So naturally I was curious about this title, I'm glad I purchased it, there's some really cool, fun and creative things going on this little gem. I'll be playing 1995 next for sure!

The devs asked me to have a cameo roll in the game so I've got to give it 5 stars right? 

Well the games so good I was going to give 5 stars anyway. 

Search YouTube for 'Pixel  Ripped 1978 by Paradise Decay for my 1st Impressions video.

This game is pure genius. I have reconnected with my gaming past! The time and detail to create this game shows passion for the industry. I’m overjoyed at this title. It is super fun, imaginative, and just pure genius. These devs obviously care about bringing great creative games to us players.

Get this! It’s amazing!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback, this is all we want to hear when creating a game. Sending a hug from the team

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_description: Pixel Ripped 1978 takes what was great about the first two games and elevates it to a whole new level. The Atari partnership allows for amazing experiences that can not be matched.

developer_response.body:
Reviews like this elevate us as well 💜
Thanks for all the love and support!

reviews.review_description: Very impressed. I can spend hours on this game 😃
This game series is so good. This will definitely give that awesome Wreck it Ralph vibe. Amazing throwback to gaming's history.
Love the nostalgic feeling that this game brings.
Like, bro, it is so hard and stupid I recommend this game NOTTTTTTT
Everything about this game speaks to the amount of adoration the devs have for the retro era. I love the "game-within-a game" gameplay and just how well made it is. Highly recommend

I am running out of words to describe the sheer genius of the developers of these pixel ripped games! I was a MASSIVE fan of the other 2 and was hugely anticipating this 3rd instalment and as usual Arvore and the team did not disappoint! Same degree of charm as the others, absolutely love the way it pays homage to the different eras of video game consoles as well as what the era was like in general when looking at surroundings etc, it’s so cleverly thought out from every single angle! Again immensely love the creative way the games cross into the real world during certain boss battles and the way you have to go into the flat version in game versus being in VR in order to progress, again a masterstroke. 

I sincerely hope there are millions more of these games to come because I could play as Dot and save the world time and time again! You guys rock and I’m already looking forward to the next one! Whenever that will be! Thank you ever so much for a mind blowing, charming, creative and ingenious experience once again! 🔥🙂

developer_response.body:
No, You Rock! Thanks for your love and support, we put a lot of love into this project and is all we wanted to hear that this is mind-blowing. 
Sending a hug from the team

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_title: slider option for fast soft turning
reviews.review_description:
The game got snap and soft turning, but the soft turning can only be done slow, games that have very fast soft turning as an option get you much less dizzyness in my opinion, fast soft turning is far better that snap turning, it just feels natural as in you don't turn yourself slowly all the time in real life, fast soft turning  is the future of VR just try it   O_O

The day will come when people look back in time and realize we today were looking around in vr at half speed, while sitting that is.
Another great story and gameplay. Hoping the next will take us into the late nineties and early 2000s for some n64 nostalgia feeling.

reviews.review_title: The Pixel Ripped Series is VR Gold
reviews.review_description:
The creativity, nostalgia, and comedic tones in the Pixel Ripped games are unmatched. The developers truly do their homework in regards to capturing the staple video game experiences of each time period they showcase. What a genius concept. There’s so many monumental time periods in video game history that this series can make new editions for years to come! 

VR has some good titles out there, but several really lame ones. Lately, most of the new release game trailers have been “meh”.  Just the same boring concepts being recycled over and over. However, whenever I see a new Pixel Ripped title, I’m immediately eager to purchase. This series’ brand has quickly made its way to the top of the VR chain in terms of quality. Without seeing a new releases trailer, a buyer can simply look at the title and confidently purchase the game expecting a cool and relatable (nostalgic) experience!

developer_response.body:
We are so happy to hear you enjoyed Pixel Ripped games. We always try to innovate and keep it fresh and exciting for you guys. Sending a hug from the team 

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_description: Great all around game, I love all the different environment you get to play in and explore, the game is very good and fun makes you want to keep playing. My favorite part is the library and the DND board game 🎯 now 😞 my grips is they missed a opportunity to go a little further by making the gold Atari cartridges actual games from Atari that you could play in an Atari emulator in one of the rooms that they made like the basement or library. That would have made people definitely get all of the golden cartridges so they could have actual entire games to play in their Atari emulator , and give it replayability. Maybe they can make this a DLC or something I know people would get it. But the game by it's self is still very good right to the end 😊 about 5 hours

developer_response.body:
We thought about emulating some Atari games but we decided to take a different root instead and explore those Ips in a way that wasn´t explored in the past. 
For example, the Pong and Breakout Boss battle moment and the bug hunt invasion on games like Food Fight, Haunted House, and Centipede. 

But thanks for your feedback, it always helps us to improve the next games. 

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_description:
Really fun, surreal game. Puzzling, challenging, and action-packed that is bunched together to probably but just possibly creating the greatest Virtual Reality video game to ever touch Meta. 
Go Buy!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! Putting us as "the greatest VR game on meta" is an honor, really 💜

reviews.review_description:
The main menu of a game can provide valuable insights about the overall experience. Unfortunately, when a monitor flies out of your face, it doesn't bode well for the game you're about to start.

First, let's address the negative points: • The 3D levels where you play as Dot feel somewhat disconnected from the rest of the game. They don't contribute much to the overall experience and seem to exist only to artificially prolong the game. There is even a level consisting solely of blue-ish cubes, which feels uninspired. • While the idea of using pixels to alter or create objects is interesting, the implementation falls short. You're limited to shooting pixels at corresponding objects without the ability to choose what you want to create or which objects you want to change. • The 2D games within the Bug's world feel more like one game separated on different cartridges. There is no variety in terms of shoot'em up or racing; it's just a platform puzzler. • The inclusion of voices from YouTubers might not be to everyone's liking. It can sometimes feel out of place or distracting.

Now, let's discuss the positive points: • The music in Pixel Ripped 1978 is truly enjoyable. The main theme and the music in the level where you fight a sock are particularly noteworthy. • The nostalgic effect of the TVs and monitors is well-executed. It truly feels like playing on an old-fashioned screen, adding to the retro charm of the game. • There are a few highlights within the game that stand out. The Arcanoid + Arcanoid battle, the Text adventure (though disappointingly short), and the art design of the last level (excluding the level design) are particularly commendable.

reviews.review_title: I give it a 5 with a favor to ask
reviews.review_description: So I love the series, so I’m gonna give you a five… but I did want to ask for one favor… please add free-locomotion in the “past scenes” if you ever make another game.  I was really hoping you would incorporate that in this game, so I was a little disappointed.  I’m dying to walk around in those nostalgic scenes, just to soak them in. For instance exploring the Atari offices would have been a dream come true, or even just to be able to walk around the 1970s living room and peek out the window. Please consider it!  Game is great but it could be legendary with that extra bit.  Obviously it’s too late for this title, but it could really increase the immersion in the next title.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharing your feedback, for this game is too late but always good to gather feedback for future games to keep improving the Pixel Ripped series.

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_description: After so many years, the gameplay formula remains original and fresh. Once again, we have likeable characters, a simple story and unfortunately, a short game length.

I'm very sad to know that the game is only in English not like PR 1995.

I will give 5 stars when you give to spanish community a good translation, of course.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, I´m sorry for that. 
We are working right now on implementing Localization. Spanish and other languages will come out really soon in the next updates.

Ana
Pixel Ripped franchise creator

reviews.review_description: Like Pixel Ripped 1995, this game will suprise you with its creativity and design. Because of some frustrating 2d platform sections, I'd rate Pixel Ripped 1995 a little higher. Overall this is must have game that takes advantage of what VR is capable of.
